title: simplify project manager routing
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- drop News project routing; NEWS-labeled items now skip the manager (label stays for social pipeline use)
- rename `tier` → `apps` in project-manager config (project ID unchanged)
- strip TIER-APP-REVIEW when approving to avoid stacked labels with TIER-APP-APPROVED
- update TRIAGE.md to match

Out-of-band follow-ups (not in this PR): rename ProjectV2 #23 title `Tier` → `Apps`, delete the unused `TIER-APP-REVIEW-PR` label.

gist.headline: Tidying the triage desk
gist.blurb: Cleaned up our internal GitHub project routing. Less label clutter means we can review community apps even faster.
gist.summary: Simplified GitHub project routing by dropping the News project manager, renaming the tier config to apps, and automatically stripping the TIER-APP-REVIEW label upon approval.
gist.impact: Internal triage is now cleaner and faster, preventing stacked labels and streamlining the community app review process.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the monitor robot organizing files or labels at a cozy triage desk, representing the cleanup of internal project routing.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-headed robot organizing glowing digital folders on a wooden desk. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes.
